mnemonics: PinHole Camera Creates Images (P-H-C-C-I) - Describes the core components of image formation.
Lens Law: 1/f = 1/d_o + 1/d_i (Just like Ohm's Law V=IR, it's a fundamental reciprocal relationship).
keyPoints: The pinhole camera model is the simplest model for image formation, projecting a 3D point to a 2D image plane.
A lens is used to gather light and form a sharper image, governed by the thin lens equation.
The field of view (FOV) of a camera is determined by the focal length and the size of the sensor.
commonMistakes: Confusing image inversion in a pinhole model; the image is always inverted.
Misapplying the thin lens equation; object distance (d_o) and image distance (d_i) are measured from the lens center.
